'''Dale Morton''' (born 31 October 1990) is a former English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played on the {{rlp|wg}}.

He previously played for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]], and [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Morton was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Knottingley Rockware and Football==
Dale Morton played for his local club [[Knottingley]] Rockware from the age of 13. He was spotted playing at a young age by Bradford and former Rockware hooker [[Wayne Godwin]]. He has also played for the Wakefield Service Area, and the Yorkshire District at Under 14s & 15s level. Morton was on scholarship with [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] until [[Wakefield Trinity]] offered him a contract at the age of 16. Clubs such as [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] and [[Bradford Bulls]] were also interested in signing Morton.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

While playing for Knottingley Rockware he helped his team win many trophies such as the Castleford & District Cup 2005. It was during the final of this competition that Dale was spotted playing against [[Castleford Lock Lane]] by current Wakefield coach [[John Kear]].{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

Before the age of 13, Morton played football for local Knottingley team [[Beal, North Yorkshire|Kellingley Welfare FC]]. From the age 8 to 13 Dale was watched by professional football clubs such as [[Leeds United F.C.|Leeds United]], [[Huddersfield Town F.C.|Huddersfield Town]] and [[Barnsley F.C.|Barnsley]]. He then opted to play rugby league with school friends and from that day has been a success in the sport.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

==First team appearances==
His début season came in 2009's [[Super League XIV]] where he played 3-games, scoring one try.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/m/morton_dale/index.php|title= Dale Morton Profile|publisher=rleague.com|date=2010-01-01|accessdate=2010-01-01}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/profile.php?id=10504|title=Dale Morton Profile @ superleague.co.uk|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2010-01-01|accessdate=2010-01-01|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110108063406/http://superleague.co.uk/profile.php?id=10504|archivedate=2011-01-08}}</ref><ref name="Salford 24-30 Wakefield"/> 
Morton made his first team début for Wakefield Trinity in a 30-24 win away to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in round 23, where he came on off the [[Rugby league positions#Substitutes|bench]].<ref name="Salford 24-30 Wakefield">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/91000/91881.php|title=Salford 24-30 Wakefield|publisher=rleague.com|date=2009-07-31|accessdate=2010-01-01|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100206021358/http://rleague.com/db/content/91000/91881.php|archivedate=2010-02-06}}</ref>
He then made his first start in a 40-28 win away at [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], a game in which he scored a try while playing on the wing.<ref name="Warrington 28-40 Wakefield">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/91000/91904.php|title=Warrington 28-40 Wakefield|publisher=rleague.com|date=2009-08-21|accessdate=2010-01-01|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100206020052/http://rleague.com/db/content/91000/91904.php|archivedate=2010-02-06}}</ref> He made his third appearance in a 24-10 win at home to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the last round of the regular season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/91000/91925.php|title=Wakefield 24-10 Hull KR|publisher=rleague.com|date=2009-09-12|accessdate=2010-01-01|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100206020648/http://rleague.com/db/content/91000/91925.php|archivedate=2010-02-06}}</ref>
On 3 October 2022, Morton scored a try for [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] in their 44-12 [[Million Pound Game]] loss to [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12710793/leigh-44-12-batley-centurions-seal-super-league-return-with-deserved-win-in-championship-grand-final/|title=Leigh 44-12 Batley: Centurions seal Super League return with deserved win in Championship Grand Final|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
